clean_body: Authenticity is the new currency in the discourse. Not big news brands. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Authenticity is the new currency in the discourse. Not big news brands. Apr 20 | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- When I first met Tucker Carlson in the summer of 2017, I sat down in front of the broad, sturdy wooden desk in his Fox News office expecting to discuss my role in spearheading the Caller's new video operation. I introduced myself and Tucker replied, "Richie McGinniss? That sounds like a famous person's name!" One thing even Tucker's adversaries can't deny after meeting him, he is a charming guy. I had just started my job at The Daily Caller, a company that he founded in 2010, while Tucker had just taken over the prime-time 8 PM slot vacated by Bill O'Reilly after some salacious revelations. Despite the notoriety of his new gig, Tucker was more interested in hearing about where I came from and what made me tick. I had just lost my father and, as an actual family man rather than pretending to be one on TV, Tucker wanted to know more about how my family was doing after the loss, and who my dad was. I mentioned that, though he was a doctor, my dad and I watched the news together every night growing up, and my dad's predictions of what would come of the Iraq War were spot on. I also told him that we often made fun of the robotic voices of the correspondents, whose on-camera personae were a far cry from their personalities in real life. I had worked at MSNBC where I should have been fired, and Tucker laughed because he was actually fired by them. Tucker was not surprised to hear that when I worked at MSNBC, the newsroom was a sterile and solemn environment. It was like the funeral home of a dying institution, while the Daily Caller newsroom was full of young people yelling at each other about every news topic under the sun. From interns to the editor-in-chief, this flat hierarchy allowed for everyone to argue their ideas as verbosely as they liked. And for those who may question how free and open the heart of this nascent media company was, just ask Geoff Ingersoll, who was my boss while I worked there and whom I put in a headlock just for fun. Even back in 2017, Tucker agreed that in a strange way the left had become the rigid and dogmatic ones, and it just so happened that the other side of the spectrum now represented the best incubator for new ideas. This brought us to the differences between the digital video and television mediums. "Joe Rogan is the father of my generation," I declared. Tucker slammed his hands on his desk in animated surprise and whipped back with his quizzically high-pitched prompt, "Really?" Perhaps it was because Rogan and Tucker are about the same age or perhaps it was because Tucker was subconsciously aware that he would one day appear on Rogan and become one of the most impactful individuals not on television, but on the world-wide web. Either way, Tucker was intrigued to hear more on why my generation and younger were tuning into podcasts rather than legacy news. "Authenticity is the currency in the discourse," Tucker summarized after I finished my Rogan spiel. That line has stuck with me ever since. A message from our sponsor (piece continues below) Major Market Shift - Act Now Before It's Too Late | | ---|---|--- 1983 - Motorola released the first commercial phone. 1992 - Nokia turned phones into personal devices. And when Apple released the first iPhone in 2007, the industry changed forever. But that was 18 years ago... Another paradigm shift is happening now, and Their EarnPhone is the only smartphone that pays users for activities like playing games, listening to music and even charging their phone. But theircurrent share price of $0.26/share is changing in under two weeks. ⚠️ (Continued) He recalled how hard he had battled to carve out autonomy in the cable news world, but given his evident lack of fear in getting fired, he always did the show his way and let the bosses upstairs decide whether he was worthy of a raise or the shit-can. In any event, Tucker refused to give up his personal voice to the corporate powers that be. And since this conversation, our media landscape has changed dramatically. New media personalities like Rogan or Theo Von were largely responsible for Trump's 2024 win. Young people came out and voted for Trump because he was a change candidate who appealed to them via the channels that they watched, rather than the ones watched by the intellectual elites. Again, the detractors can say what they want about Carlson, but if their accusations are to mean anything, then they must at least acknowledge that he says what he really thinks. And therein lies the reason why Tucker has become so successful despite the repeated efforts by media Goliaths like the New York Times to take him down. As our discourse is transposed from the old pillars of information--print or TV or radio--to the new platforms on the internet, audiences trust big media brands less. Instead, they are choosing to trust individuals that they deem to be authentic and acting in good faith. The agenda-driven narratives that write the scripts in cable television and in the newsrooms of traditional outlets are finally being seen for what they are: corporate programs that care more about keeping their audience as captive consumers rather than telling them the truth about why America is becoming more sad and afraid by the day. Here's a hint: this business plan has more to do with pushing pharmaceuticals, cheap consumer goods, and fruitless foreign wars than informing you on who really pulls the strings and why. The Democrats' Summer of Riots Exposed: Check out my book for my firsthand account covering the left-wing riots of 2020 that the media told you were "peaceful protests." FYI, they weren't... Get your info directly from the source, not from mainstream media giants trying to sell you an agenda. | | ---|---|--- While I was covering the widespread protests and riots in 2020, this institutional inclination to tell viewers what they wanted to hear rather than what really happened became more evident than ever before. One side of the landscape only showed BLM activity when it was "mostly peaceful" while the other only focused on the most violent aspects that proved the Black Lives Matter demonstrations were inherently bad. Regardless of where one fell on that spectrum, the pandemic and the protests made it blatantly obvious to the American public that the gatekeepers of our discourse don't give a shit about anything other than keeping multinationals like Pfizer happy, because it's through their advertising dollars that the news commentators are exorbitantly paid. What I learned after witnessing the lockdowns and the violence during the so-called "summer of love", then covering the so-called "Capitol Insurrection" from the inside, is that the overwhelming majority of people in media are putting forth opinions that are aggregated from groupthink rather than a byproduct of their genuine beliefs. That's why Tucker was an enigma in the space, repeatedly sharing opinions that were not in line with what the executives wanted him to say. Whether it was his questions about Dominion voting machines or January 6 instigators that ultimately got Tucker fired, we may never know. But what we do know is why people followed Tucker on his new foray into digital media: it's because people trust that no matter what the topic, Tucker will say what he truly thinks. At its very heart, the success of Tucker's new program has to do with one thing: He is intellectually curious at a time when the rest of the media is not. He is independently minded and willing to change his views over time while the rest of America's thought leaders have become rigid and set in their ways. We find Joe Rogan and Tucker Carlson now in adjacent political camps. Not because they agree on everything, but because they have no concern with maintaining the status quo, and care more about exploring new frontiers. The rules of the political media game are changing before our eyes. For the last century or so, this game has been refereed by the monolithic mouthpieces paid by massive corporations. But as with the printing press, the advancement of technology has shifted the paradigm and allowed anyone to jump into the arena of public debate. We used to have our Daily Caller Christmas party at one of the bougeiest clubs in Washington, DC, the Metro Club. Everyone would get dressed to the nines and I, being somewhat of a contrarian, would come up with a new way to violate the dress code every year. The first year I snuck in wearing sandals that said FAKE NEWS (close toed shoes are required for men). The second year I sauntered in wearing a Saudi Keffiyeh on my head and used my four years of Arabic training to convince the management that I was a wealthy Saudi and therefore they could not force me to remove my headdress (hats are not allowed inside the club). The third year I showed up with a fistful of hand-rolled, home-grown doobies in a suit jacket pocket. When I got the award for "Funniest Caller" I came up to accept the certificate with one of the doobies in my mouth. As Tucker handed it over he laughed, "weed is lame!" "I grew this and rolled it myself!" I replied. "OK that's awesome," he laughed. Tucker was open about his own struggle with vices in the past, at once both relatable and willing to admit how he had changed his ways. He looked me in the eye and paid attention to me while we spoke, rather than looking around the room for more important people than I. This cannot be said about the overwhelming majority of people who work in DC media or politics; they are more interested in kissing ass than hearing new ideas. Those are the people who still run cable news, and that approach to the rapidly changing world is why cable news is dead. PolitiBrawl is a reader-supported publication. name: D.O.G.E. Task Force
subject: President Trump didn’t come back to play nice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Conservative Battleground Fund PAC (conservativebattlegroundfund
body:
Peter,

President Trump didn't come back to play nice with the bureaucrats.

_He returned with a wrecking ball and brought D.O.G.E. with him._

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d21ed46b-ab8a-40ed-b6db-0f25bf25aff8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This week I did a press interview with a reporter who writes for a Chilean newspaper. As we chatted, I was struck by the extent to which Chile may very well be Ground Zero of [the current war between China and the United States](https://substack.com/redirect/b9df26aa-a6d7-4874-849f-8f40b04a638d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). As Powers Boothe's character says in Red Dawn when asked about the origins of the movie's fictional U.S.-Soviet war, "Two toughest kids on the block, I guess. Sooner or later, they're gonna fight."
Since the election of President Donald Trump last fall, the Trump Administration has been aggressively rethinking and resetting virtually all spheres of domestic and foreign policy. Aware that the clock is ticking, and trying to make up for lost time, it seems as if Trump is trying to remake the U.S. and the world in only a matter of months. It's an ambitious and unconventional approach, one that is raising eyebrows the world over. Amidst the many cross-pressures and confusing contradictions created by this approach, one objective seems to be increasingly clear: remove Chinese influence and control from the Western Hemisphere. As I have written [before](https://substack.com/redirect/11d842bf-f194-4513-a32d-16b12cec66ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Trump is intent on restoring the Monroe Doctrine.
* * *
## [America's Evolving Global Role](https://substack.com/redirect/d41a5d77-77e4-4ad5-9b16-d7c81bd48d7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/profile/37278065-vikram-mansharamani)| | *
---|---|---
| Feb 27
[](https://substack.com/redirect/d41a5d77-77e4-4ad5-9b16-d7c81bd48d7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[Since the day he descended an escalator to upend American politics, President Donald Trump has consistently delighted his supporters and confounded his critics. Seemingly on separate planets, these t...](https://substack.com/redirect/d41a5d77-77e4-4ad5-9b16-d7c81bd48d7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/d41a5d77-77e4-4ad5-9b16-d7c81bd48d7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
In the short run, the America First agenda seems to be driving a wedge between the U.S. and Latin America, a dynamic that appears to be at odds with isolating China. Some analysts are asking, "Why send South American countries into the welcoming arms of China?" Others are taking a more realpolitik approach, noting that the U.S. is more important to the global economy than China, and, in fact, without a destination for its goods, China is a paper tiger.
Reconciling these two perspectives is a matter of timing. For years, I've been writing and [speaking](https://substack.com/redirect/854d779a-1407-4328-9d04-3b5fba755111?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the fact that the [U.S.-China war](https://substack.com/redirect/b9df26aa-a6d7-4874-849f-8f40b04a638d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is bifurcating the world into two global economic ecosystems. One system, led by the United States and the Western World, is based on a belief in individualism, rule of law, democracy, property rights, freedom, liberty, and faith in the human ability to innovate. The other, led by China, is focused on the collective, an authoritarian approach to imposing what is best for the group.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..eAv4uSi9VDbMT2srB9x0-WOKTRZIO_LjmSpJ3zXdL8E?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=161752064)
To navigate the cross-currents of a bifurcating world, many countries are trying to "hedge" their bets by refusing to pick sides. This sentiment is best captured by a conversation I had in the Middle East in 2019, roughly a year before the pandemic. When suggesting this country would have to pick either the U.S. or China, the minister replied, "We don't want to do that. We want to sell our oil to the Chinese and are happy to keep our wealth in dollars so we can get U.S. military protection." I pushed back, suggesting that eventually his country would have to choose. He insisted his country wouldn't ...which is when I asked him if they would be upgrading their telecom networks anytime soon, and if so, whether he'd be using Huawei equipment. Or, if China insisted on paying in Renminbi, would they accept payment? The conversation got tense...
**Not picking a side will increasingly be a dangerous strategy** , and despite the short-term anxiety over tariffs, thoughtful leaders will understand they need to choose.
Trump is clearly signaling that the U.S. will not tolerate interlopers in our backyard, and his administration has been aggressively pushing China out of the Americas. This policy has already born [fruit](https://substack.com/redirect/1839e68b-becc-411d-aba1-16ac829ccbf0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as many Latin American countries are following suit. Mexico is cracking down on transshipment and other methods China uses to get its products into the U.S., the U.S. reasserted its role in the Panama Canal, and Guatemala and Honduras are exploring greater relations with Taiwan; in the case of Honduras, this is an abrupt reversal, after severing ties altogether just two years ago.
One country seemingly caught in the middle of the U.S.-China tension is Chile. China is the top importer of Chilean products, accounting for around [40%](https://substack.com/redirect/290ade4c-d7f8-4161-81f3-cd527fba294d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Chilean exports. China is also making significant investments in Chile's mining sector, and a Chinese company [owns](https://substack.com/redirect/45f3f7ad-5371-4e58-8a9c-3eff2bcfd63a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a large stake in Sociedad Quimica y Minera, the largest lithium producer in the world.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d2d60dec-d06a-4cc2-bbb2-85a92ee544b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
SQM Chile. Source:_[The Economic Times](https://substack.com/redirect/971afbb4-3cac-488c-a80a-67cba1fbfed8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
All of this makes Chile important in the proxy war between Far East and West, but if there is one thing that makes it worthy of global attention, it's copper. Copper is a critical component in electronics, datacenters, and electric vehicles. This makes securing the metal essential in terms of economics and national security, and Chile has roughly [23 percent](https://substack.com/redirect/2ed48509-2397-4702-8aa1-106d01e8356b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of the world's supply.
China and the U.S. are Chile's top copper customers. China, always starved for natural resources, buys over [70 percent](https://substack.com/redirect/0777a549-60fa-4152-8b39-9a9aa69bf6e0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Chile's copper exports. Chile is also the top supplier of copper to the United States, and there have been warnings that we could be facing a [shortage](https://substack.com/redirect/9fc1d7b3-fe06-4b23-869f-e4338e3521f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the near future. The mineral has grabbed the attention of the Trump Administration and now risks getting caught up in the trade war frenzy.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e9369515-e004-4bab-b61c-cd2f89d3504e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Escondida Mine, Chile; Source:_[The Mining Journal](https://substack.com/redirect/9bd9e41d-ce37-4900-b563-8fb78d9d41e1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
The U.S. is _**considering**_ levying tariffs on copper imports from Chile, and last February Trump has ordered an investigation on national security grounds. Chile is pushing back, arguing in a [letter](https://substack.com/redirect/67493c4d-ae57-4886-b8e0-7b886df032d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the Trump Administration that Chilean copper imports don't threaten U.S. national security; in fact, just the opposite. Since the majority of Chile's copper exports go to China, U.S. tariffs may very well end up benefitting the Chinese; the supply available to the Chinese would increase and prices would go down. The Chileans argue that this is what would undermine U.S. national security.
Chile's argument perfectly sums up the two cross-pressures of competing U.S. aims: push China out of the Western Hemisphere and arrest the trade imbalances across various trading partners through the use of tariffs. An unintended consequence of these two goals has been that America's neighbors in North and South America are actively considering the benefits of trade with China.
Sooner or later the two toughest kids on the block are going to fight, and Chile will have to pick a side. And Chileans have to realize that whichever side it picks, it will have to make concessions. So, it can accept Monroe 2.0, make trade concessions to the U.S., and participate in an economic and political ecosystem that respects liberty, property rights, and the rule of law, or it can run headlong into the arms of a various consumer of Chilean exports while accepting the risk that Chile may get muscled by Chinese investment, debt bombs, and domestic subversion. Either way, Chile will bear a cost to its choice and the country will have to make concessions.
As Milton Friedman once said, "There's no such thing as a free lunch."

**VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. He's currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, _**[The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World](https://substack.com/redirect/30d9e2d7-6786-4d2f-8d5f-3eddc14a3d1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ , _**[Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence](https://substack.com/redirect/037251c9-f8a8-4dac-a6de-d94ab7b0331e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_ and _**[Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst](https://substack.com/redirect/ebd8def9-fa5e-4c0c-bb44-67a2a77de0a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**_. Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.
You're currently a free subscriber to [Navigating Uncertainty](https://substack.com/redirect/f75c141d-60e1-43b5-95b9-6a85aee67d7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Picking Sides in the US-China War | | | Apr 20 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- This week I did a press interview with a reporter who writes for a Chilean newspaper. As we chatted, I was struck by the extent to which Chile may very well be Ground Zero of . As Powers Boothe's character says in Red Dawn when asked about the origins of the movie's fictional U.S.-Soviet war, "Two toughest kids on the block, I guess. Sooner or later, they're gonna fight." Since the election of President Donald Trump last fall, the Trump Administration has been aggressively rethinking and resetting virtually all spheres of domestic and foreign policy. Aware that the clock is ticking, and trying to make up for lost time, it seems as if Trump is trying to remake the U.S. and the world in only a matter of months. It's an ambitious and unconventional approach, one that is raising eyebrows the world over. Amidst the many cross-pressures and confusing contradictions created by this approach, one objective seems to be increasingly clear: remove Chinese influence and control from the Western Hemisphere. As I have written , Trump is intent on restoring the Monroe Doctrine. | | | | ---|---|--- | Feb 27 | In the short run, the America First agenda seems to be driving a wedge between the U.S. and Latin America, a dynamic that appears to be at odds with isolating China. Some analysts are asking, "Why send South American countries into the welcoming arms of China?" Others are taking a more realpolitik approach, noting that the U.S. is more important to the global economy than China, and, in fact, without a destination for its goods, China is a paper tiger. Reconciling these two perspectives is a matter of timing. For years, I've been writing and about the fact that the is bifurcating the world into two global economic ecosystems. One system, led by the United States and the Western World, is based on a belief in individualism, rule of law, democracy, property rights, freedom, liberty, and faith in the human ability to innovate. The other, led by China, is focused on the collective, an authoritarian approach to imposing what is best for the group. To navigate the cross-currents of a bifurcating world, many countries are trying to "hedge" their bets by refusing to pick sides. This sentiment is best captured by a conversation I had in the Middle East in 2019, roughly a year before the pandemic. When suggesting this country would have to pick either the U.S. or China, the minister replied, "We don't want to do that. We want to sell our oil to the Chinese and are happy to keep our wealth in dollars so we can get U.S. military protection." I pushed back, suggesting that eventually his country would have to choose. He insisted his country wouldn't ...which is when I asked him if they would be upgrading their telecom networks anytime soon, and if so, whether he'd be using Huawei equipment. Or, if China insisted on paying in Renminbi, would they accept payment? The conversation got tense... Not picking a side will increasingly be a dangerous strategy , and despite the short-term anxiety over tariffs, thoughtful leaders will understand they need to choose. Trump is clearly signaling that the U.S. will not tolerate interlopers in our backyard, and his administration has been aggressively pushing China out of the Americas. This policy has already born , as many Latin American countries are following suit. Peter,
Every week under Trump's second term brings a new wave of outrages. But this week? It was something else entirely.
This is the type of constitutional crisis we used to talk about as a warning. Now, it's just... a typical week:
* The Fed says Trump's tariffs are driving up inflation.
* RFK Jr. is contradicting the CDC and pushing junk science.
* Federal jobs are being slashed right and left.
* Programs like Head Start and public broadcasting are on the chopping block.
* The administration is openly looking into sending American citizens to prisons in other countries.
* The FDA will be ending most of its routine safety inspections...
This isn't policy—it's punishment. And we can't let it go unchallenged.
GOTV PAC is laser-focused on defeating Trump Republicans at every level—governor's races, state legislatures, down-ballot fights—because we know this isn't just about Washington anymore.
It's about fighting to preserve basic rights and protections across the country.

body:
---
Derek, We’re about to ask you to [donate directly to the DNC.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-april-2025?refcode=em_20250420_3219_fr}&amount=3&refcode2=3219_22930333_HJdpVQ&akid=3219%2E22930333%2EHJdpVQ) If you give us a moment of your time, we’ll explain why your support is so important right now._ Here’s the situation: Trump is imposing a trillion-dollar consumer tax on working families, sending the global economy into a tailspin, and is actively working to dismantle our government and democracy as it stands. **There has never been a more important time for Democrats to take action, and the DNC isn’t sitting back.** We’re suing Trump over his illegal voter suppression efforts and his attempted takeover of the FEC. We’ve set up a new War Room operation to combat the far-right’s lies, and we’ve launched the People’s Cabinet, an initiative that brings together experts and community leaders who will cut through the lies and speak directly to the American people about the destruction of the Trump administration. That’s on top of our ongoing work to organize _everywhere_ , year-round, in every ZIP code. But the truth is, these programs take a lot to keep running. When we hit our fundraising goals, we’re able to allocate resources effectively — ensuring these programs remain strong and successful and that we can continue the fight against Trump’s extremism. **That’s why reaching our fundraising goals is so important. name: Katie Porter for Governor 2026
subject: 2026 is around the corner
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=39655%2E3422442%2EBxF7Mw)
Dear Derek,
We know there are a lot of causes and candidates where you can spend your money. We want to take a quick second to explain why you should donate to us.
1. **
Katie will be on the ballot next year.** We aren’t stockpiling cash for some hypothetical race in the future. We’re going to put your donation to good use communicating with voters across California—soon.
2. **
This race is competitive.** With 10+ declared candidates running and a majority of voters undecided, this race is a true toss up. Your dollars will make a real difference.
3. **
California needs strong political leadership.** The path to the House majority runs through California. And our candidate at the top of the ticket—the governor’s race—has an essential role to help us secure the down ballot turnout we need to retake the House.

We don’t take your contributions for granted, and we wouldn’t ask if it weren’t important. **[Will you chip in today to make a difference in the 2026 election, when we have so much on the line?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250420_1_ak?refcode=em20250420-39655&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39655_3422442_BxF7Mw&akid=39655%2E3422442%2EBxF7Mw)**
[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250420_1_ak?refcode=em20250420-39655&refcode2=39655_3422442_BxF7Mw&akid=39655%2E3422442%2EBxF7Mw)
Thanks for reading,
Team KP26

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20250420-39655&amounts=&t=3&refcode2=39655_3422442_BxF7Mw&akid=39655%2E3422442%2EBxF7Mw)**

name: Wiley (personal)
subject: From my family to yours
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina**
body:
| Peter, As we embrace the warmth and renewal of this season, I want to extend my heartfelt wishes to you and your loved ones. This time of year is a beautiful reminder of the importance of rebirth, unity, and celebrating life’s blessings. For those who celebrate, Easter is a special time for reflection, joy, and coming together. It’s a chance to enjoy the company of family and friends and to find happiness in the simple moments that connect us. With everything going on in our busy lives and the challenges we face, it’s so important to take a moment to appreciate the joys around us. I hope you find peace, love, and happiness in your celebrations, and that this season brings you renewed hope and inspiration. To share a bit of our celebration, here’s a photo of my family — my wife, Caroline, and our two amazing children, Adeline and Prescott — the reason why I’m fighting so hard for a brighter future. Let's keep the spirit of togetherness and gratitude alive not just today, but every day. Your support and commitment to our vision for North Carolina truly inspire me and together, we can create the future we all deserve. From my family to yours — we wish you a joyous celebration and many blessings. Wiley, Caroline, Adeline, and Prescott

clean_body: | Peter, As we embrace the warmth and renewal of this season, I want to extend my heartfelt wishes to you and your loved ones. This time of year is a beautiful reminder of the importance of rebirth, unity, and celebrating life’s blessings. For those who celebrate, Easter is a special time for reflection, joy, and coming together. It’s a chance to enjoy the company of family and friends and to find happiness in the simple moments that connect us. With everything going on in our busy lives and the challenges we face, it’s so important to take a moment to appreciate the joys around us. I hope you find peace, love, and happiness in your celebrations, and that this season brings you renewed hope and inspiration. To share a bit of our celebration, here’s a photo of my family — my wife, Caroline, and our two amazing children, Adeline and Prescott — the reason why I’m fighting so hard for a brighter future. Let's keep the spirit of togetherness and gratitude alive not just today, but every day. Your support and commitment to our vision for North Carolina truly inspire me and together, we can create the future we all deserve. From my family to yours — we wish you a joyous celebration and many blessings. Wiley, Caroline, Adeline, and Prescott | = Wiley Nickel for North Carolina PO Box 563 Raleigh, NC 27602 United States | mailto:info@wileynickel.com | Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina

name: Team FLHP
subject: $10 can make a huge difference
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC**
body:
Our April fundraising deadline is just 10 days away, and this is the perfect time to ensure we have the resources we need to elect Democrats up and down the ballot. |
#
---
Team, Democrats showcased some real momentum earlier this month. Susan Crawford overcame more than $25 million spent against her to win a seat on the state Supreme Court, proving that GOP dark money donors are no match for Democratic grassroots energy. Democrats also made strong inroads in two deep-red Florida Congressional districts that Donald Trump won by more than 30 points and showed that the GOP’s extremist agenda is becoming more unpopular by the day. And Cory Booker inspired millions across the country when he spoke for more than 25 hours straight on the Senate floor and lifted up the voices of countless individuals who have been impacted by Republicans in Washington, D.C. **We have real power when we work together and _fight like hell_ for what we believe in. Now, we need to make sure we build on our momentum in the weeks and months ahead.** Important races are happening this year in states like New Jersey and Virginia, and the midterm elections will be here before we know it. Fight Like Hell PAC will be there every step of the way to elect Democratic candidates who will deliver real progress for working families and defend our fundamental freedoms. [**Our April fundraising deadline is just 10 days away, and this is the perfect time to ensure we have the resources we need to elect Democrats up and down the ballot. Susan Crawford overcame more than $25 million spent against her to win a seat on the state Supreme Court, proving that GOP dark money donors are no match for Democratic grassroots energy. Democrats also made strong inroads in two deep-red Florida Congressional districts that Donald Trump won by more than 30 points and showed that the GOP’s extremist agenda is becoming more unpopular by the day. And Cory Booker inspired millions across the country when he spoke for more than 25 hours straight on the Senate floor and lifted up the voices of countless individuals who have been impacted by Republicans in Washington, D.C. We have real power when we work together and fight like hell for what we believe in. Now, we need to make sure we build on our momentum in the weeks and months ahead. Important races are happening this year in states like New Jersey and Virginia, and the midterm elections will be here before we know it. name: Team Schrier
subject: Chasing 0.00625% fraud is not oversight—it’s a distraction.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Kim Schrier for Congress
body:
This isn’t fraud reform. It’s sabotage. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106196400/540721057/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMGRiMjc0Yy00MjFlLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lb30ZU0Q5P0WqttMix8dWgDyid0UJLcC-OUHp4PmkKA=&emci=3c0d9191-681c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=10db274c-421e-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=15820338)
---

Peter,

The Trump administration is once again launching an assault on Social Security and Medicare.

Under the guise of fighting fraud, they are weaponizing bureaucracy and delay, hurting everyday Americans who rely on these earned benefits. But let’s be clear. . .

_**This is a solution in search of a problem.**_

Out of the $1.6 trillion the government distributes each year in Social Security benefits, just _0.00625%_ is lost to fraud. That’s a minuscule amount. For decades, strong oversight systems have been in place to safeguard these earned benefits—**and they work.**

The changes being pushed now won't solve real issues— _but they will make it harder for people to get the help they need._ Instead of investing in customer service or reducing wait times, the Trump team is expanding legal reviews that waste time and resources.

**This is not about accountability. It’s about making it harder for seniors, veterans, and people with disabilities to access the benefits they’ve paid into and earned.**

Kim will never stop fighting for the benefits you've earned, and she'll always stand up for the millions of Americans who depend on Social Security—but she needs you with her every step of the way. **[Add your name if you believe we should protect and strengthen Social Security, not destroy it under the false pretense of reform.>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/106196401/540721058/-1538004039?ms=em25_250420_ssm_adv_gq_act_PET_TS_ettx&&&&refcode=em25_250420_ssm_adv_gq_act_PET_TS_ettx&&&&source=em25_250420_ssm_adv_gq_act_PET_TS_ettx&&&&utm_campaign=em25_250420_ssm_adv_gq_act_PET_TS_ettx&&&&utm_medium=email&&&&contactdata=ateDo34S9T0HjfDNjPyIsZD5mzB5ilVXFMJvqn6KBfMCnov87p6vLE8kgC2c2otyAxb8QAQv1zejOihksz8QOsRm4gdDukfGDN2XwCsApXK/h3NEU0PIxeRtNC0hRZ4oZaOvNx5zYhO4chwUwyBd/CXjsTXNfS0RLLfzmCji6+E=&&&&refcode2=10db274c-421e-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxMGRiMjc0Yy00MjFlLWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Lb30ZU0Q5P0WqttMix8dWgDyid0UJLcC-OUHp4PmkKA=&emci=3c0d9191-681c-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=10db274c-421e-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=15820338)**
